_comment: Example passtest configuration demonstrating mask attacks and rule-based dictionary attacks. Copy and adapt to your case. Rule file paths are resolved relative to this file if not absolute.

jobs._comment: MASK — All 4-digit PINs: 0000–9999. Fast, worth trying first.
MASK — 6-digit PINs and short numeric codes (000000–999999).
MASK — Capitalised word of 5 lowercase letters + 2 digits (e.g. Pippo12).
MASK — Same pattern with a trailing special character (e.g. Pippo12!).
MASK — 6 lowercase + 2 digits, no capital (e.g. pippo12).
MASK — Custom charset (-1 = digits + common symbols); 5 lowercase + 3 chars from that set.
DICT — Plain dictionary, no rules. Catches exact passgen candidates.
DICT — best64.rule: 64 common mutations (append digits, toggle case, reverse, etc.).
DICT — Two rule files stacked: best64 mutations then case-toggle variants.
DICT — SHA-512 mode with best64 rules, on a third GPU.
